name: mobile-mcp
description: Model Context Protocol Server for Mobile Automation and Scraping (iOS, Android, Emulators, Simulators and Real Devices)
readme_summary: The Mobile MCP server acts as a platform-agnostic interface for automating and interacting with native iOS and Android applications. It allows agents and LLMs to control mobile devices (emulators, simulators, and real devices) using accessibility data or screenshot-based analysis, simplifying mobile development and automation tasks.
key_features: Platform-agnostic interface for iOS and Android automation
Supports emulators, simulators, and real devices
Interacts with native applications via accessibility snapshots or coordinate-based taps
Enables LLM-driven automation and agent-to-agent communication for mobile tasks
Fast and lightweight with fallback to screenshot analysis when accessibility data is unavailable
primary_use_case: To provide a unified, platform-agnostic interface for automating native iOS and Android applications on various device types, enabling LLM-driven interactions and complex user journeys.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled conductor standing at a podium, orchestrating a symphony of mobile devices (represented by stylized smartphones and tablets). The conductor's baton, glowing with digital energy, directs streams of data flowing from the devices to a central AI brain depicted as a wise, glowing orb. The scene is a futuristic, minimalist stage with subtle UI elements showing accessibility trees and code snippets integrated into the background. The overall mood is one of seamless control and intelligent automation, with a clean, vibrant color palette. The image should be in a 3D isometric illustration style, emphasizing clarity and the interconnectedness of mobile automation.

name: certimate
description: An open-source and free self-hosted SSL certificates ACME tool, automates the full-cycle of issuance, deployment, renewal, and monitoring visually. 完全开源免费的自托管 SSL 证书 ACME 工具，申请、部署、续期、监控全流程自动化可视化，支持各大主流云厂商。
readme_summary: Certimate is an open-source, self-hosted ACME tool that automates the entire lifecycle of SSL certificate management, including issuance, deployment, renewal, and monitoring. It offers a user-friendly GUI, zero dependencies, and low resource usage, making it suitable for various deployment scenarios.
key_features: Automated SSL certificate issuance, deployment, renewal, and monitoring
Self-hosted with full data control and privacy
Zero dependencies, single-click deployment
Low resource usage (~16MB memory)
User-friendly GUI for visual workflow orchestration
Supports single/multiple/wildcard domain and IP certificates
Supports RSA or ECC key types
Supports DNS-01 and HTTP-01 challenges
Supports PEM, PFX, JKS certificate formats
Integrates with 60+ domain registrars
Integrates with 110+ deployment targets
Multiple notification channels (email, Slack, Discord, etc.)
Supports multiple ACME CAs (Let's Encrypt, ZeroSSL, etc.)
primary_use_case: Automating and simplifying the management of SSL certificates for self-hosted environments through a visual interface.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, anthropomorphic robot gardener meticulously tending to a vibrant, glowing tree that represents an SSL certificate. The robot is shown watering the roots (representing issuance), pruning branches (deployment), and gently adjusting the leaves (renewal), ensuring the tree remains healthy and secure. Small digital particles representing data flow around the tree. In the background, a stylized, simplified cityscape with secure, glowing connections between buildings hints at the network. The repository name 'Certimate' is subtly integrated into the robot's chassis. The overall mood is one of automated care and security. 3D isometric illustration style with a clean, bright color palette and soft lighting.

name: OpenMemory
description: Local persistent memory store for LLM applications including claude desktop, github copilot, codex, antigravity, etc.
readme_summary: OpenMemory is a self-hosted, local-first cognitive memory engine for AI agents, providing real long-term memory beyond simple embeddings. It offers SDKs for Python and Node.js, integrates with popular agent frameworks, and supports data ingestion from various sources.
key_features: Real long-term memory for AI agents
Self-hosted, local-first storage (SQLite/Postgres)
Python and Node.js SDKs
Integrations with LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, Streamlit, MCP, VS Code
Connectors for GitHub, Notion, Google Drive, OneDrive, Web Crawler
Explainable traces for memory recall
primary_use_case: To provide AI agents and LLM applications with persistent, long-term memory, enabling them to retain information and context across interactions without relying solely on stateless models or basic RAG.
cover_image_prompt: A wise, ancient librarian meticulously organizing glowing, ethereal wisps of knowledge into a vast, interconnected library. Each wisp represents a piece of an AI's memory, being carefully cataloged and stored on shelves that extend into infinity. The librarian uses a special magnifying glass to examine the wisps, ensuring their context and relevance. Subtle digital particles and faint code snippets float in the background, hinting at the underlying technology. The library is dimly lit, with warm light emanating from the organized memories. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration, emphasizing depth and the organized structure of memory.

name: sglang
description: SGLang is a high-performance serving framework for large language models and multimodal models.
readme_summary: SGLang is a high-performance serving framework designed for efficient inference of large language models (LLMs) and multimodal models. It offers advanced features like optimized attention mechanisms and supports a wide range of popular models, enabling faster and more scalable AI deployments.
key_features: High-performance inference for LLMs and multimodal models
Optimized attention mechanisms (e.g., FlashAttention, PagedAttention)
Support for a wide array of open-source models (Llama, Mistral, Qwen, etc.)
Day-0 support for new model releases
Multi-GPU and distributed inference capabilities
Support for various backends including CUDA and Jax (TPU)
primary_use_case: Serving large language models and multimodal models with high performance and low latency for inference.
cover_image_prompt: A bustling, futuristic city where towering skyscrapers represent different large language models. A central, highly efficient transport hub, resembling a sleek, glowing train station, is SGLang. Trains, representing user requests, are rapidly and smoothly arriving and departing from the hub, carrying glowing data packets. The hub itself is a nexus of light and energy, with subtle UI elements showing optimized data flow and speed metrics. The surrounding city is vibrant but slightly less organized, highlighting SGLang's role as a streamlined, high-performance gateway. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a clean, futuristic aesthetic and bright, energetic lighting.

name: x402
description: A payments protocol for the internet. Built on HTTP.
readme_summary: x402 is an open standard for internet-native payments, built on HTTP. It aims to support all networks and forms of value, abstracting away complexities like gas fees for clients and resource servers.
key_features: Open standard for internet-native payments
Supports all networks (crypto & fiat) and forms of value (stablecoins, tokens, fiat)
Built on HTTP, complementing existing data transportation
Network, token, and currency agnostic
Backwards compatible
Trust minimizing
Easy to use, abstracting crypto complexities
primary_use_case: Enabling seamless, network-agnostic payments over the internet using HTTP.
cover_image_prompt: A bustling futuristic marketplace where diverse digital currencies (represented by glowing orbs of different colors and symbols) and physical currencies (represented by stylized coins and bills) flow effortlessly through a central, transparent HTTP pipeline. Merchants (resource servers) and customers (clients) interact seamlessly via sleek, minimalist interfaces. Small, subtle UI elements showing transaction confirmations and network logos are visible. The overall scene is clean, bright, and conveys ease of use and universal compatibility, with the 'x402' logo subtly integrated into the pipeline's design. Style: 3D isometric illustration with vibrant colors and a focus on clear, intuitive iconography.
